name: Marina Green

permits.description: remodel (e) member bar. new electrical, plumbing, and lighting. new built-in furnishings and fixtures at bar area. new service equipment in drinks prep area.
erect 1 story, type 5-b, public restroom bldg.
perform earthquake repairs to structure remodel

barrier removal including; renovation of office space & shower rms & restrooms. work includes; mod. to door openings w/assoc. modifications to slab & diaphragm, reconfiguration of toilets & shower, incl. plumbing & elec.; repair to int. & ext. finishes; & (n) int. lighting and signage.
structural reinforcement of building. (e) structure is falling apart
accessible barrier removal: (n) plumbing fixtures, painting, electrical & associated work, (n) floor finish, accessible path of travel & paving work in parking lot & new accessible drinking fountain.

summary_en: Marina Green appears in the city's records as an active waterfront park and event ground. A 2011 draft environmental impact report for the 34th America's Cup proposes an "AC Village" at Marina Green. Recreation and Park Commission minutes index a Marina Green bear-vending contract, a Marina Green report, and "No Parking Marina Green," while Operations Committee agendas approve tented events and an overnight charity walk on the green. A 1993 Doyle Drive Task Force report describes the bayfront recreation corridor "from the Marina Green to Fort Point," and 1980s parks bulletins list outdoor art shows on the green. Marina Green is operated by the Recreation and Park Department, whose FY2025 budget line is about $250M.
